footnote:
Note: Originally, MTR releases included a Maintenance support phase. Support phases for MTR releases now end when the next version is released.

Note: The Migration Toolkit for Runtimes product will reach its End of Life by September 30th, 2024. All customers using it should start their to transition to Migration Toolkit for Applications, as it is fully backwards compatible with all features and rulesets available in Migration Toolkit for Runtimes and will be maintained in the long term.

footnote:
Note: All future dates mentioned are close approximations, non definitive, and subject to change.

Any Red Hat build of Keycloak release version is tested on the latest current and previous OCP 4.x minor updates that are available at the time of that given release. Older versions of OCP 4.x are also supported as long as they are under active support according to the OpenShift life cycle. However customers are highly encouraged to always use the most recently available version of Red Hat OpenShift to take advantage of the latest features, capabilities, and security improvements. Due to potential incompatibility issues or known limitations (e.g changes in Kubernetes APIs, unsupported features, etc.), customers may be required to upgrade to the tested/certified versions of OCP 4.x in order to continue receiving support.

Additional Helpful information

  • Red Hat build of Keycloak Life Cycle and Support Policies
  • Red Hat Application Services Product Update and Support Policy
  • OpenShift Operator Life Cycles

all_phases.tooltip: This is an Add-On entitlement. Customers must have this entitlement on their account in order to receive this support.
all_phases.display_name: 延長ライフサイクルサポート (ELS) アドオン

all_phases.name: 延長ライフフェーズ
all_phases.tooltip: The Extended Life Cycle Phase (ELP) is the post-retirement time period. We require that customers running Red Hat Enterprise Linux products beyond their retirement continue to have active subscriptions which ensures that they continue receiving access to all previously released content, documentation, and knowledge base articles as well as receive limited technical support. As there are no bug fixes, security fixes, hardware enablement, or root cause analysis available during the Extended Life Phase, customers may choose to purchase the Extended Life Cycle Support Add-On during the Extended Life Phase, which will provide them with critical impact security fixes and selected urgent priority bug fixes.
